items_en: 2. International Conventions. (a) The State shall be bound by all international agreements and conventions, of a general or particular nature, to which Palestine has become a party. These agreements and conventions shall be requspected by the State throughout the period for which they were concluded, subject to any right of denunciation which such agreements may provide.

(b) Any difference relating to the continued applicability or validity of national conventions or treaties of which the Acting Power is a signatory or to which it has acceded for Palestine shall be brought before the International Court of Justice, in accordance with the provisions of the Statute of the Court.

3. Financial obligations. (a) The State shall respect and execute all financial obligations, of whatever order, assumed in the name of Palestine by the proxy Power during the exercise of the mandate and recognized by the State.

(b) The State shall comply with those obligations, which are applicable to the whole of Palestine, by participating in the Joint Economic Council; it shall individually comply with those which, applicable to States, may be equitably distributed among them.

(e) A Court of Resellers, attached to the Joint Economic Council and comprising a member appointed by the United Nations, a representative of the United Kingdom and a representative of the State concerned, shall be established. Any difference between the United Kingdom and the State concerning claims not recognized by the latter shall be submitted to that Court.

(d) Trade concessions have been granted for any part of Palestine, before the adoption of the resolution by the General Assembly, shall be maintained in accordance with the terms of the agreements, unless they are amended by agreement between the holder of the agreement and the State.

items_en: 14. Special powers of the Governor with regard to holy places, buildings or sites in the City and in the region of Palestine. (a) The Governor shall be particularly concerned with the protection of holy places, edifices and religious sites in the City of Jerusalem.

(b) With regard to such places, buildings and sites of Palestine outside the City, the GooE shall decide, by virtue of the powers conferred upon it by the Constitution of both States, whether the provisions of the Constitutions of the Arab State and of the Jewish State of Palestine relating to such liquities and the religious rights thereof shall be duly applied and respected.

In these functions, the Governor may be assisted by an advisory board composed of representatives of different denominations serving in an advisory capacity.
